problem: April has four different basil plants and four different tomato plants. In how many ways can she arrange the plants in a row if she puts all the tomato plants next to each other?

classified_questions.question: What is the problem asking us to find?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many basil plants does April have in total?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many tomato plants does April have in total?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: If all the tomato plants are placed next to each other, how many plants are there in the group?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can we treat the group of tomato plants as a single entity? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we arrange the group of tomato plants in a row?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If we treat the group of tomato plants as a single entity, how many "objects" are left to arrange?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we arrange the remaining basil plants and the group of tomato plants (as a single entity)?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we combine the arrangements of the basil plants and the group of tomato plants separately? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the total number of ways to arrange the plants given the arrangement of the group of tomato plants and the remaining basil plants?
classified_questions.label: b
